bibliographic_data.title: Maximum pairwise-rank-likelihood-based inference for the semiparametric transformation model
bibliographic_data.abstract: In this paper, we study the linear transformation model in the most general setup. This model includes many important and popular models in statistics and econometrics as special cases. Although it has been studied for many years, the methods in the literature are based on kernel-smoothing techniques or make use of only the ranks of the responses in the estimation of the parametric components. The former approach needs a tuning parameter, which is not easily optimally specified in practice; and the latter is computationally expensive and may not make full use of the information in the data. In this paper, we propose two methods: a pairwise rank likelihood method and a score-function-based method based on this pairwise rank likelihood. We also explore the theoretical properties of the proposed estimators. Via extensive numerical studies, we demonstrate that our methods are appealing in that the estimators are not only robust to the distribution of the random errors but also lead to mean square errors that are in many cases comparable to or smaller than those of existing methods.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes two new methods for estimating parameters in the linear transformation model: a pairwise rank likelihood method and a score-function-based method based on this pairwise rank likelihood.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors propose using pairwise rank likelihood to estimate the parametric components of the linear transformation model, which is more robust than existing kernel-smoothing techniques. They also develop a score-function-based method that leverages the pairwise rank likelihood for improved efficiency.
outcomes: Proposed methods are robust to the distribution of random errors
Mean square errors of proposed estimators are comparable or smaller than those of existing methods
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we study the linear transformation model in its most general setup. The model includes many important and popular models in statistics and econometrics as special cases. Existing methods based on kernel-smoothing techniques require a tuning parameter that is difficult to optimally specify, while rank-based methods use only the ranks of responses and may not fully utilize data information. We propose two new methods: a pairwise rank likelihood method and a score-function-based method based on this pairwise rank likelihood. Through extensive numerical studies, we demonstrate that our proposed estimators are robust to random error distributions and have mean square errors comparable or smaller than existing methods.
